title: Kalaivaniye Song Lyrics Sindhu Bhairavi
stanzas:
Kalaivaniye
unaithaane alaithaen
uyir theeyai valarthaen
vara vaendum
varam vaendum
thudithaen thozhuthen
palamurai ninaithaen
azhuthaen isai tharum (2)
Kalaivaniye

thuram paadi siriththaai
sirippaale erithaai
madi meedhu marithaen
maru jenmam koduthai
siru viralgalil thalai koathi
madithanil enai valarthaai
isai enum varam varum neram
dhisai solla villai marandhaai

mugam kaatta maruththaai
mugam kaatta maruththaai
mugavariyai maraithaai
nee mun vandhu
poochindhu vizhith thuligal
therikkiradhu thudaithuvidu

Kalaivaniye
unaithaane alaithaen
uyir theeyai valarthaen
vara vaendum
varam vaendum
thudithaen thozhuthen
palamurai ninaithaen
azhuthaen isai tharum
Kalaivaniye

Ullam azhuthadhu
unnai thozhuthadhu
unadhu uyril ivan paadhi

gangai thalaiyinil
mangai idaiyinil
sivanum ivanum oru jaadhi

raman oru vagai
kannan oru vagai
irandum ulagil sama needhi

angae thirumagal
ingae kalaimagal
avalum ivalum sari paadhi

kanneer perugiyathe...
kanneer perugiye
kannil un mugam
azhagiya nilavena midhakkum

uyire.. uyirin uyire
azhagae.. azhagin azhagae
ini azha valuvillai
vizhigalil thuli illai
ini oru pirivillai
thuyar vara vazhiyilla
varuvaai ....

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of the Kalaivaniye song?

The song is devotional, expressing a deep yearning for divine grace and musical inspiration.

Who composed the music for Sindhu Bhairavi?

The music was composed by the legendary Ilaiyaraaja.

Who sang Kalaivaniye?

The song was soulfully rendered by K.J. Yesudhas.

What is the significance of the song in the movie?

The song reflects the protagonist's devotion and quest for musical excellence, central to the film's narrative.

When was Sindhu Bhairavi released?

The movie was released in 1985.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Kalaivaniye from Sindhu Bhairavi is a heartfelt devotional melody that captures the protagonist's deep longing for divine inspiration and musical fulfillment. Set against the backdrop of a classical music theme, the song evokes a sense of spiritual yearning and emotional depth. Ilaiyaraaja's composition, combined with K.J. Yesudhas's evocative vocals, creates a soothing and reflective atmosphere. The song plays a pivotal role in the film, highlighting the protagonist's inner journey and artistic struggles.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the divine melody of Kalaivaniye from the classic film Sindhu Bhairavi. This song beautifully blends Ilaiyaraaja's musical genius with K.J. Yesudhas's soulful voice, creating an unforgettable devotional experience. Dive into the soothing rhythms and let the music transport you to a place of peace and reflection.
stanzaMeanings: Oh Goddess of Arts, I called you, nurtured the fire of life, I need you to come, I need a boon, I prayed and worshipped many times, I thought and cried, you who give music.
You sang from afar and smiled, your smile burned me, I died on your lap, you gave me a new life, you raised me with your fingers on my head, the boon of music is coming, but you forgot to guide me.
You refused to show your face, you hid your address, you came before me, flowers fell, tears are flowing, wipe them away.
Oh Goddess of Arts, I called you, nurtured the fire of life, I need you to come, I need a boon, I prayed and worshipped many times, I thought and cried, you who give music.
The heart cried, worshipped you, half of my life is yours.
Ganga on the head, Parvati on the waist, Shiva and he are of the same kind.
Rama is one way, Krishna is another way, both are equal justice in the world.
There, the goddess of wealth, here, the goddess of arts, both are equally important.
Tears are overflowing... tears are overflowing, your face is beautiful like the moon in my eyes.
Life... life of life, beauty... beauty of beauty, there is no more crying, no more tears in the eyes, no more separation, no path for sorrow, come...
